cases.sql: CREATE TABLE test_table (c1 INT, c2 DATE) PARTITION BY (c2)
cases.reason: brikk extension #9: Doris CREATE TABLE requires a partition-definition list after PARTITION BY (cols); we emit `PARTITION BY (c2) ()` (kind-less = LIST per the FE, partitions added later), sqlglot re-emits the rejected bare form

cases.sql: CREATE TABLE test_table (c1 INT, c2 DATE) PARTITION BY (c1, c2)
cases.reason: brikk extension #9: Doris CREATE TABLE requires a partition-definition list after PARTITION BY (cols); we emit `PARTITION BY (c1, c2) ()`, sqlglot re-emits the rejected bare form

cases.sql: CREATE TABLE test_table (c1 INT, c2 DATE) PARTITION BY (DATE_TRUNC(c2, 'MONTH'))
cases.reason: brikk extension #9: a function partition key is only analyzer-valid as (auto) RANGE in Doris; we emit `PARTITION BY RANGE (DATE_TRUNC(c2, 'MONTH')) ()`, sqlglot re-emits the rejected bare form

cases.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW test_table (c1 INT, c2 INT) KEY (c1)
cases.reason: brikk extension #10: Doris MV column lists take bare column names (simpleColumnDef); we emit `(c1, c2)`, sqlglot re-emits typed column defs the FE parser rejects
